heading: Impact adjustment lending programs
label: 262p Impact adjustment lending programs
notes: Codificationis based on section 701 of title VII of H.R. 3750, One Hundredth Congress, as introduced , and enacted into law by .Section 1601 of Pub. L. 95–118Pub. L. 100–202Dec. 11, 1987
DefinitionsThe definitions in apply to this section.section 262p–5 of this title

children.heading: Establishment of guidelines; impact statements
children.label: a Establishment of guidelines; impact statements

children.text: guidelines be established which reflect clear and tangible concern for the impact adjustment lending programs, and the activities in support of which such lending is made, have and will have on human welfare; and

impact statements be required which assess the effect an adjustment lending program, and the activities in support of which such lending is made, will have on the poor of the country to which such lending is made.

children.heading: Proposed contents of impact statements
children.label: b Proposed contents of impact statements

children.text: specify what the projected effects of the adjustment loan will be on the poor;

children.label: A
children.text: monitor nutrition levels in a timely manner; and

children.label: B
children.text: measure the impact an adjustment loan, and the policies and activities in support of which such loan is made, has on the living standards of the country’s population, especially the poorest; and

children.label: A
children.text: mitigate any adverse effect the policies and activities in support of which an adjustment loan is made are expected to have on the living standards of the poor (including the use of the proceeds of any adjustment loan, project aid, or other compensatory measure to mitigate such effect); and

children.label: B
children.text: maximize the extent of the participation of the poor in the economic benefits resulting from an adjustment loan.

children.heading: Report to member governments by United States Executive Director of International Bank for Reconstruction and Development and by International Development Association
children.label: c Report to member governments by United States Executive Director of International Bank for Reconstruction and Development and by International Development Association

children.text: assesses the impact on the poor of structural adjustment in countries to which structural adjustment lending has been made; and

children.label: A
children.text: mitigate any adverse effect of adjustment lending, and the activities in support of which such lending is made, on the living standards of the poor in the countries to which such loans are made; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure the participation of the poor in the economic benefits resulting from adjustment lending and the activities in support of which such lending is made.

children.heading: “Adjustment lending” defined
children.label: d “Adjustment lending” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section and , the term “adjustment lending” means nonproject lending in support of structural macroeconomic reforms or sectoral economic reform.section 262m–1 of this title
